data.title: B. Riley initiates Lionsgate Studios at Neutral, sets $17 price target
data.description:
B. Riley Securities initiated coverage of Lionsgate Studios (LION) with a Neutral rating and a price target of $17, citing improving operating fundamentals but saying the stock's recent rally leaves its valuation appearing full.

New PT represents about 11% upside from the stock's last close at $15.31 on Tuesday.

The brokerage said Lionsgate's Motion Picture business is supported by the strong box office performance of Michael, a film pipeline anchored by franchises including The Hunger Games, John Wick, and The Resurrection of the Christ, and a recovery in the theatrical market.

It also expects the Television Production segment to rebound in fiscal 2027, driven by higher scripted episodic deliveries, stable library revenue, and easier year-over-year comparisons.

B. Riley forecast FY27 revenue and adjusted OIBDA growth, alongside improving free cash flow that it said should support a gradual reduction in leverage over time. However, it flagged leverage as a near-term constraint, noting about $1.94 billion in long-term debt and net leverage of 6.1 times entering fiscal 2027.

The firm said acquisition speculation is likely to remain part of the investment narrative following Lionsgate’s separation from Starz, while adding that the timing of any potential transaction remains uncertain. It based its $17 price target on a 17.2-times multiple of its fiscal 2027 adjusted OIBDA estimate.

data.title: Fed Chair Warsh demurs on forward guidance as part of charting new course
data.description:
"We're going to chart a new course," Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh said on Wednesday,  responding to a request for forward guidance. That contrasts with his predecessor's practice of indicating which way policymakers were leaning in upcoming meetings.

The Fed has received a lot of data, he said, and the Federal Open Market Committee members will debate their views at the July meeting. "I take their views seriously," he said on a panel at the European Central Bank's Forum on Central Banking.

He agreed with a statement made earlier by Bank of Canada Governor Tiff Macklem. "We're all being hit by a series of shocks," Warsh said, though he declined to say whether the inflationary effects of those shocks would be temporary.

"We recommit to delivering price stability," he added.

Some economists and policymakers expect productivity from AI developments to reduce inflationary pressures, while others say increased AI capital expenditures are inflationary. 

Warsh expects to see that spending boom eventually reflected in supply. Increased supply would have “huge” implications for policy, he added. "I'd certainly rather have this problem of massive capital expenditures" than prior periods when corporations were focused on financial engineering, Warsh said.

The rate of change in AI model improvement is "exponential," he noted.

"The United States is not afraid of productivity-led economic growth," he said. However, it's not a “zero-sum” situation, where productivity-led growth in the U.S. is going to take away from productivity elsewhere

Labor markets are steady, demand in the economy is solid, and the supply side is strong, Warsh said.

Expectations of inflation over the last four weeks have come down, and inflation risks have come down. He says anyone who expected the Fed to be content with inflation above its target "will be disappointed," Warsh said.

"The most important thing we can do is to get monetary policy right," he said. If the Fed's communications policy is an obstacle to that, then it should get rid of it, he said.

"This is a rare moment to go back to first principles" and question how the Fed does things, Warsh added.

Warsh said the Fed will likely disclose next week who will be the outside experts on the five task forces that he is creating to review the central bank's practices.

Developing… Check back for updates.

data.title: Sandisk slumps even as BofA ups price target on growing supply-demand imbalance
data.description:
• Sandisk (SNDK) shares slumped more than 8% in early trading on Wednesday even as Bank of America upped its price target on the memory and storage company, citing the growing supply-demand imbalance.
  • The firm raised its price target to $2,500 from $2,100 and reiterated its Buy rating.
  • “We expect supply/demand imbalance in the NAND market to remain through C27 and expect pricing to remain strong for longer (through mid-2027), albeit growing at a lower q/q percentage rate over time,” Bank of America analyst Wamsi Mohan wrote in a note to clients. “For the June quarter, we model bit growth of 13% q/q and ASP growth of 35% q/q. This is higher than the NAND bit growth reported recently by competitor Micron Technology, but lower than the q/q ASP growth reported by that company. The level of price increase that SNDK sees will be determined by mix and by other factors such as the level of NBM contracts that are signed in the quarter (pricing for these are fixed in the near-term, but more variable in the longer-term).”

data.title: Stocks to watch on Wednesday: KLAR, META, KR
data.description:
Stock index futures (SPX) fell slightly early Wednesday, signaling a muted start to the new month after equities posted solid gains over the first six months of the year.

Here are the three stocks to watch on the day:

Klarna (KLAR) shares jumped 6.7% in premarket trading after a Swedish court ruled in favor of its subsidiary, awarding $1.97B in damages in an antitrust case against Google. The award compensates for lost revenue resulting from Google's preferential treatment of its own comparison-shopping service over independent price-comparison services. Klarna acquired PriceRunner in 2022, and the ruling is subject to appeal by Alphabet-owned Google, with any ultimate payments reduced by sharing arrangements with former PriceRunner shareholders and litigation funders.

Meta Platforms (META) shares  jumped more than 6% in premarket trading following reports that the company is building a cloud business to monetize excess artificial intelligence compute. According to reports, Meta is forming a business unit known internally as Meta Compute, with plans that include selling access to various AI models hosted on its infrastructure, similar to Amazon Web Services' Bedrock approach. CEO Mark Zuckerberg previously told investors at the company's shareholder meeting in May that selling excess compute was under consideration, stating "It's definitely on the table."

Kroger (KR) fell 1.1% in premarket trading after announcing it will acquire Giant Eagle, one of the largest privately held grocery chains in the U.S., for $1.65B. The deal includes $1.25B in cash and the assumption of approximately $400M in outstanding liabilities. Giant Eagle generates approximately $9B in annual sales across 197 supermarkets and 11 standalone pharmacies located in northern Ohio, western Pennsylvania, West Virginia, Maryland, and Indiana, with the transaction expected to close in 2027.

data.title: Benchmark refreshes 'best ideas' roster
data.description:
Benchmark Research on Wednesday released its quarterly update to its "best ideas" list for July 2026, highlighting 31 of its analysts' highest-conviction, catalyst-driven stock picks across major market sectors.

In this latest refresh, the firm made a few notable adjustments to its curated portfolio. Benchmark added Pinnacle Financial Partners (PNFP) to its Financials selections. Over in the Consumer sector, real estate technology firm AppFolio (APPF) and packaged food maker Freshpet (FRPT) were newly tapped as top picks, with Freshpet directly replacing Chef's Warehouse (CHEF), which was removed from the roster entirely.

The full breakdown of Benchmark's highest-conviction ideas, organized by Seeking Alpha Quant system ratings, is detailed below:

  • Dave, Inc. Class A (DAVE) - Quant Rating: 4.90 — Sector: TMT / Industry: FinTech

data.title: Most shorted stocks over $2B market cap
data.description:
Wolfspeed (WOLF) topped the list of the most heavily shorted U.S.-listed stocks with market capitalizations above $2B, with 53.77% of its shares outstanding sold short. The semiconductor maker was followed by SoundHound AI (SOUN) at 40.21% and Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA) at 36.01%.

The list is dominated by application software and biotechnology companies, sectors that tend to attract elevated short interest because of their higher valuations, ongoing cash burn, regulatory uncertainty, or dependence on future growth.

Here's the list of the most heavily shorted U.S. stocks with market capitalizations above $2B:

  • Wolfspeed (WOLF): 53.77%
  • SoundHound AI (SOUN): 40.21%
  • Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA): 36.01%
  • SELLAS Life Sciences (SLS): 34.06%
  • NETSTREIT (NTST): 33.72%
  • CleanSpark (CLSK): 33.04%
  • Ondas Holdings (ONDS): 33.00%
  • Hims & Hers Health (HIMS): 29.35%
  • Beam Therapeutics (BEAM): 28.44%
  • Twist Bioscience (TWST): 28.36%

These ETFs best align with the dominant themes represented by the most-shorted stocks: Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ), Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK), Vanguard Information Technology ETF (VGT), SPDR S&P Biotech ETF (XBI), iShares Biotechnology ETF (IBB), iShares Russell 2000 Growth ETF (IWO), Vanguard Small-Cap Growth ETF (VBK), Valkyrie Bitcoin Miners ETF (WGMI), Bitwise Crypto Industry Innovators ETF (BITQ), and VanEck Digital Transformation ETF (DAPP).

data.title: New Era Energy & Digital names Nelson as Chairman and CEO
data.description:
New Era Energy & Digital (NUAI) said Wednesday it appointed Charlie Nelson, currently President and COO, as its new Chairman and CEO, effective immediately, as part of a broader leadership reshuffle at the data center developer.

Current Chairman and CEO E. Will Gray II will transition off the board and into the role of President of the Permian, where he will continue to support New Era's (NUAI) Permian Basin relationships.

The company also named Ted Warner as President and CFO and appointed him to its board and tapped José Rodriguez as COO after he held data center and infrastructure roles at Microsoft, TikTok, and Amazon Web Services.

data.title: Salesforce climbs after Guggenheim upgrades to Buy
data.description:
Shares of Salesforce (CRM) climbed about 4% premarket on Wednesday after Guggenheim upgraded the stock to Buy from Neutral with a $228 price target, noting that "AI will pressure CRM, but not kill it."

Analysts led by John DiFucci said they upgraded the shares because they believe the company's current valuation presents an attractive entry point for investors.

"Our view on CRM remains unchanged: despite being a leading AI marketer, neither product traction nor material revenue contribution is yet visible in our channel conversations or in the reported numbers. Shares have declined nearly 41% YTD on fears of an impending "SaaSpocalypse." While we do see AI as a significant risk and view CRM as a company likely to be negatively impacted by the advent of Agentic AI, we believe the Armageddon scenario currently priced into the stock is misaligned with reality," said DiFucci and his team.

The analysts added that more realistically, they underwrite a scenario in which the company struggles to grow much but does not decline much either, while the current stock price implies that it declines 5% into perpetuity.

"To be clear, this is not a call that CRM will be a beneficiary of AI, but we don’t believe it will decline as implied in the current valuation," said the analysts.

data.title: Micron secures long-term agreement with GM as SCAs become broader revenue strategy
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU) has secured a long-term Strategic Customer Agreement with General Motors (GM) to supply the U.S. automaker with memory and storage platforms necessary for vehicle production, the companies announced today.

It stands as one of 16 such Strategic Customer Agreements, or SCAs, that Micron executives discussed during the company's third quarter fiscal 2026 conference call last week. Micron said SCAs now account for about 40% of its business, and eventually half of the business will be tied to SCAs.

"So, I'll start by saying that these strategic customer agreements, or SCAs, cannot be canceled," said Sumit Sadana, Micron's executive vice president and chief business officer, during the call. "Now there is no provision in this agreement to enable the customer or allow a customer to walk away from this agreement. These are designed to be take-or-pay agreements outside of automotive. Generally, these are 5-year agreements. There are annual volume commitments for each of those years. And the take-or-pay means that whether they want to purchase the bits or not, they are obligated to pay for the price times the volume."

Micron said the SCA with GM is enabled in part due to its domestic investments, including $2B towards an advanced DRAM manufacturing fab in Manassas, Va., which entered production this year.

"As demand for memory and storage continues to grow, we are investing to extend supply availability, expand capacity and align more closely with our customers to improve supply predictability across the automotive ecosystem," said Micron CEO Sanjay Mehrotra. "Our expanding manufacturing efforts in the United States are designed to enable GM to deliver both near-term products as well as secure U.S.-based supply to support next-generation platforms and innovation."

data.title: Forget Spirit, JPMorgan says Allegiant Travel has a winning model
data.description:
JPMorgan initiated coverage of Allegiant Travel Company (ALGT) on Wednesday with an Overweight rating.

Analyst Jamie Baker said the demise of the ultra-low-cost carrier model has been widely reported given their paucity of premium, international, and loyalty, which has driven a sustainable advantage for legacy carriers. Spirit's second bankruptcy filing in a 12-month span was called the most notable proof point. However, while many LCCs remain profit deficient, Allegiant (ALGT) is noted to have posted consistent operating margins competitive with, and at times topping, industry leaders. 

Baker and his team believe Allegiant (ALGT) has written the playbook for the next generation of LCCs,  built on lower frequency and off-peak utilization to underserved secondary markets. The firm also sees meaningful upside following the Sun Country Airlines (SNCY) acquisition.

JPMorgan assigned a price target of $156 to ALGT. "Given the above-peer margins/margin durability, growth, and clean balance sheet, we believe a premium to the legacy peer set, similar to pre-COVID, is warranted," wrote Baker.

Shares of Allegiant Travel (ALGT) were up 0.5% in premarket action to $118.21.
